rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEONARD RUSSELL MORECI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/21/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) If the cash on hand is to be distributed equally to the three heirs, how will the attorney fees be paid? (2) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed, including the complete legal description, street address (if any), and assessor's parcel number (if any) of real property. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] The legal description of real property must also be in the order for final distribution, which will be recorded to transfer the property. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/7/26. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The other two heirs waived notice for all proceedings, but had notice been required, the incorrect form of notice was served. The only correct form of notice for the petition is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. Also, notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL J. TEDESCHI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/15/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Notice was mailed to Madelane Dixon at an address in Rhode Island, not California. (3) Paragraph 10 of the petition alleges that the executor took no action under the IAEA, but in paragraph 16, she reports that she liquidated all assets for cash. (4) The executor was appointed on 9/10/24, not 10/15/24. Deadline for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues is 4/1/26. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] The copy of the amended petition received by the examiner was not a true and complete copy of the amended petition; Madelane Dixon's waiver of accounting was not attached to it. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Trusteeship And Compel Disclosure Of Trust Records 17200, 16060-16061

[LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) No courtesy copies have been received by the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE DORIS PHILLIPS TRUST DATED JUNE 10, 2005 AS AMENDED JUNE 21, 2016
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Account, For Return Of Funds, To Remove Trustee, For Surcharge, And For Attorney'S Fees (Pc 17200, 16063, 850, 16440, And 15642)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/14/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) No notice of hearing has been filed with the court. Form DE-115 is the mandatory notice for this petition (Probate Code Section 850). 2) For the court to make an order appointed a successor trustee, the petition must name a successor who is willing to act. 3) A petition involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries (CRC 7.902). 4) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings and a proposed order to this examiner no later than 04/01/2026 at avoid additional continuance.
